//: Branch protection for main as a native GitHub REPOSITORY RULESET (H6).
This file IS the in-repo record of protection intent — the Probot
settings.yml `branches:` block it replaces never synced (app not
installed; main was still unprotected on 2026-07-04).
FIRST apply (no ruleset named 'main' exists yet):
UI:  Settings → Rules → Rulesets → New … → Import a ruleset → this file
API: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets -X POST --input .github/rulesets/main.json
RE-apply (ruleset exists — POST 422s with 'Name must be unique'; UI import
also only creates). Update in place instead:
ID=$(gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="main") | .id')
gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets/$ID --input .github/rulesets/main.json
Either way needs repo Administration: write (fine-grained PAT, not
GITHUB_TOKEN). Verify: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rules/branches/main
Fleet-wide application is automated by infra/github-governance (Pulumi);
keep this file and that stack's config in sync — they encode the same intent.
Required-check contexts: reusable-workflow check runs are named
'<caller job name> / <callee job name>' — hence 'Review gate / gate' and
'PR title check / check'. The ci.yml jobs keep single-segment names.
Path-skipped ci.yml jobs still satisfy required checks (skipped counts).
No required_approving_review_count rule: graduated review (owner decision
2026-07-04, #681) — automerge.yml routes risk-labeled / proto-touching
PRs to humans instead of a blanket approval gate.
The repo-level toggles that used to live in settings.yml (allow
auto-merge, delete branch on merge) are NOT ruleset material — flip them
once under Settings → General; see docs/runbooks/ecosystem-governance.md.
name: main
